checks.a_analysis: Comparing the output text to the source PDF, all paragraphs and sentences in the JSON are derived directly from the source material. No content has been invented or fabricated by the pipeline.
The source document contains no phone numbers, email addresses, or URLs. The output correctly reflects this absence and does not fabricate any contact details.

All primary sections of the minutes, including the participant lists and agenda items I through IV, are present in the output. The missing footer text and approval stamp are considered omissions of specific text lines rather than entire missing sections.

The document contains lists of names rather than formal tables. These lists (Members Present, Staff Present, etc.) have been accurately converted to JSON arrays with the correct number of items matching the source.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The body text of all meeting items is complete. While the footer and a red approval stamp are missing, they do not account for a significant portion (over 25%) of any individual section or the document overall.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Several minor discrepancies exist: 1) The output corrects a typo in the source ('Chariman' to 'Chairman' in Item I). 2) The large red approval stamp 'APPROVED BY NC 911 BOARD 08/23/19' is omitted. 3) The footer text containing the approval date ('...as Approved August 23, 2019...') is omitted. These represent a loss of specific information (the approval date) present in the original document.
checks.b_evidence_source: I. Call to Order – Chariman Boyette...; APPROVED BY NC 911 BOARD 08/23/19; NC 911 Board Minutes for August 14, 2019, as Approved August 23, 2019
checks.b_evidence_output: I. Call to Order – Chairman Boyette...; [stamp omitted]; [footer omitted]
checks.b_evidence_location: Item I (reading_order 14), Red stamp overlay, and Page Footer

checks.a_analysis: The output JSON does not contain any duplicated content blocks. Each section of text from the source document is represented exactly once in the structured output.

The heading hierarchy is logical and follows a standard progression (H1 for title, H2 for meeting name, H3 for sub-sections like attendance). There are no skipped levels or incorrectly nested headings.

The source document uses a two-column layout for attendance lists rather than a formal data table. The pipeline correctly identified these as distinct lists under relevant headings rather than forcing them into a table structure, which is appropriate for accessibility.

The reading order in the output JSON follows the logical flow of the document. It proceeds from the header information to the attendee lists (processing the left column then the right column) and finally to the meeting minutes body. This matches the intended flow for a screen reader.

The source document contains a numbered list using Roman numerals (I through IV). In the output, these items are rendered as separate paragraphs (indices 14-18) rather than as a semantic list structure. Additionally, Section III in the source contains two distinct paragraphs; in the output, the second paragraph (index 17) is rendered as a standalone paragraph equal in level to the numbered headings, losing its structural association with the 'III.' list item.
checks.b_evidence_source: I. Call to Order – Chariman Boyette called the meeting to order at 6:00 PM. ... III. Senate Bill 600 - Chairman Boyette opened with discussion... [New Paragraph] Mr. Greene made the motion to approve...
checks.b_evidence_output: Element index 14: "I. Call to Order...", Element index 16: "III. Senate Bill 600...", Element index 17: "Mr. Greene made the motion..."
checks.b_evidence_location: Main body of minutes, element indices 14 through 18.

checks.a_analysis: There are no links in the source document, and no links have been injected into the output.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output correctly translates the multi-column layout of the source PDF into a single-column responsive layout. The organization of information—starting with meeting details, followed by attendees, and then the numbered minutes items—matches the source. Although the watermark is removed and lists are stacked vertically, these are intentional or expected differences in HTML conversion.
The source document does not contain any formal tables. The lists of attendees, which are presented in a two-column layout in the PDF, are correctly rendered as standard lists in the HTML.
The logos present in the source PDF (at the top left and bottom center) are captured and rendered in the HTML output, located at the end of the document. All meaningful images are present.

No body content (paragraphs, lists, or sections) is duplicated. The repetition of headings in the 'Contents' sidebar and the main body is an intentional accessibility feature and not considered a defect.
